name: Extremadura (Spain)

colors.symbolism: the pastures and fields of Extremadura
purity and the historic faith of the region
the soil, the black cattle of Extremadura, and the memory of historic suffering

description: Three equal horizontal stripes of green, white, and black from top to bottom.
history: Designed in the late 1970s during the transition to autonomy and adopted as the official flag of Extremadura with the Statute of Autonomy of 25 February 1983. The state flag (Bandera Oficial) adds the Extremaduran coat of arms in the centre.

features.symbolism: Green for the fields and dehesas of Extremadura, white for purity and faith, and black evoking both the dark cattle of the region and a tradition of mourning recalling Extremaduran losses; the colours also echo those used by the historic Order of Alcantara.
